mfa.enforcement: Required for all users accessing PHI; FIDO2 required for controlled substance workflows

enterprise_sso.federation: Cloud Identity Platform federating with Active Directory and Okta

authentication.accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA Level 3.3.8 compliance — no CAPTCHAs, accessible authentication flows
authentication.step_up_auth: Step-up authentication (WebAuthn/TOTP) required for PHI access and controlled substance operations (PA review, EPCS)

key_rings.phi_key_ring: AES-256 keys for PHI data (Cloud SQL, FHIR Store, BigQuery)
key_rings.operational_key_ring: AES-256 keys for operational data (Memorystore, Cloud Storage)
key_rings.ai_artifacts_key_ring: AES-256 keys for AI model artifacts and evaluation data

secrets_management.api_keys_and_credentials: Secret Manager for API keys, database credentials, third-party integration secrets
secrets_management.ibmi_credentials: HashiCorp Vault for IBM i connection credentials (IWS, ODBC, Precisely Connect)
secrets_management.rotation_policy: 90-day automated key rotation for all CMEK keys; 30-day rotation for service account credentials

network_isolation.vpc_service_controls: Perimeter around all HIPAA-regulated services (Cloud SQL, FHIR Store, BigQuery, Vertex AI, Cloud Storage)

hybrid_connectivity.primary: Partner Interconnect with IPsec encryption (1-2 Gbps, upgrade path to Dedicated Interconnect at 10 Gbps)
hybrid_connectivity.backup: HA VPN (IPsec) for redundancy and failover

network_tiers.public: Cloud Armor WAF, Global Load Balancer, Apigee X gateway
network_tiers.private: Cloud Run services, Cloud SQL, Memorystore (VPC-native, no public IPs)
network_tiers.isolated: IBM i on-premises network segment, Partner Interconnect termination

network_isolation.waf: Cloud Armor with OWASP Top 10 rules, Adaptive Protection ML, IP allowlisting for partner APIs
network_isolation.microsegmentation: Firewall rules enforcing deny-all default with explicit allow per service-to-service path; separate VLANs for API, CDC, and management traffic

ai_guardrails.dlp_gate: Cloud DLP synchronous blocking gate before GenAI inference — detects and blocks PHI in assembled prompts
ai_guardrails.de_identification_pipeline: Automated de-identification of clinical notes before AI processing using Cloud DLP info types
ai_guardrails.hitl: Human-in-the-loop pharmacist review mandatory for all AI-generated PA recommendations — no autonomous AI actions
ai_guardrails.confidence_thresholds: Recommendations with confidence >= 0.8 route to standard queue; below 0.8 route to senior pharmacist review
ai_guardrails.structured_output: Gemini controlled generation enforcing JSON schema output — no free-text fields where PHI could leak
ai_guardrails.owasp_llm: Controls aligned with OWASP Top 10 for LLMs (prompt injection, data poisoning, model theft, etc.)

compliance.hipaa: HIPAA §164.312 technical safeguards — access control, audit controls, integrity, authentication, transmission security
compliance.hitech: HITECH Act breach notification requirements and increased penalty enforcement
compliance.pci_dss: PCI-DSS 4.0 for pharmacy payment card processing (tokenization, strong access control)
compliance.dea_epcs: DEA 21 CFR 1311 for electronic prescriptions of controlled substances — identity proofing, two-factor authentication, audit trails
compliance.soc2: SOC 2 Type II — logical access controls, system monitoring, change management
compliance.nist_zero_trust: NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture — BeyondCorp principles, verify explicitly, least privilege

threat_model.scope: End-to-end CVS Health Legacy System Transformation including IBM i AS/400 hybrid integration, GCP cloud services, GenAI PA recommendation pipeline, CDC data synchronization, and cross-cloud Azure myPBM API surface

threats.description: IBM i *ALLOBJ/*SECADM authority profiles mapped directly to cloud IAM roles without downscoping. Legacy profiles carry broad system-level privileges that violate least-privilege in the cloud context.
threats.mitigation: Create a formal authority-to-IAM translation matrix mapping each IBM i special authority to specific GCP IAM roles with resource-level bindings. Run automated daily reconciliation comparing IBM i profile authorities against cloud IAM grants. Alert on drift exceeding defined thresholds.

threats.description: SAML assertion replay attack against Cloud Identity Platform. An intercepted SAML assertion could be replayed to gain unauthorized access to PHI-containing services.
threats.mitigation: Enforce SP-initiated SSO only (reject IdP-initiated flows). Maintain assertion ID replay cache in Memorystore Redis with 5-minute TTL matching assertion lifetime. Validate NotOnOrAfter and NotBefore conditions server-side. Require signed assertions with SHA-256.

threats.description: OAuth device code phishing targeting pharmacy staff. Attackers send phishing messages with device codes, tricking staff into authorizing attacker-controlled sessions with PHI access.
threats.mitigation: Disable OAuth device code grant type in Cloud Identity Platform configuration. Restrict all clients to Authorization Code flow with PKCE. Conduct quarterly security awareness training for pharmacy staff on phishing vectors. Deploy phishing-resistant FIDO2 keys for high-privilege users.

threats.description: GCP service account key exfiltration for genai-pipeline@ and cdc-pipeline@ accounts. Exported JSON keys could provide persistent access to Vertex AI endpoints and CDC data pipelines.
threats.mitigation: Enable iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation org policy constraint. Use Workload Identity Federation for all service-to-service authentication. Enforce VPC Service Controls perimeter to prevent token exfiltration. Monitor for anomalous service account usage via Cloud Audit Logs.

threats.description: JWT algorithm confusion attack against the BFF service. An attacker submits a JWT with alg:none or alg:HS256 using the public key as HMAC secret to bypass signature verification.
threats.mitigation: Centralize all JWT validation at the Apigee X gateway with explicit algorithm pinning to RS256. Reject tokens with alg:none or any symmetric algorithm. Remove JWT parsing logic from application-layer services entirely. Validate issuer, audience, and expiration claims.

threats.description: CDC journal entries modified in transit at the EBCDIC-to-UTF-8 transformation point. The Precisely Connect pipeline converts IBM i journal data through a character encoding boundary where tampering could alter clinical or financial values.
threats.mitigation: Apply HMAC-SHA256 on CDC messages at the IBM i source before EBCDIC conversion. Verify HMAC at Cloud-side CDC consumer after UTF-8 transformation. Run nightly hash reconciliation between IBM i source records and Cloud SQL target. Enforce VPC Service Controls perimeter around CDC pipeline.

threats.description: Claims adjudication data manipulation targeting pricing fields (copay, coinsurance, deductible). A compromised service or insider could alter financial amounts during the claims processing workflow.
threats.mitigation: Apply JWS message signing using Cloud KMS on all claims payloads at the Apigee gateway. Enforce OpenAPI schema validation with bounds checking on numeric fields. Maintain immutable claims audit trail in BigQuery with pre/post values. Enable change data capture on Cloud SQL claims tables.

threats.description: GenAI prior authorization recommendation manipulation. An attacker or compromised component alters AI-generated PA recommendations between the Vertex AI inference endpoint and the pharmacist review interface.
threats.mitigation: Apply cryptographic signing (Cloud KMS) to all pipeline outputs at the Vertex AI inference boundary. Enforce VPC Service Controls around the entire Vertex AI project. Implement ensemble disagreement detection between Gemini primary and MedGemma secondary models to flag anomalous outputs.

threats.description: Formulary data tampering through unauthorized tier changes. Malicious modification of drug tier assignments could affect copay calculations for millions of members.
threats.mitigation: Implement event sourcing with immutable append-only log for all formulary changes. Require dual-control approval (two authorized users) for tier modifications. Run nightly hash reconciliation between formulary source of truth and operational copies. Alert on any out-of-band modifications.

threats.description: Audit log tampering to cover tracks after a security incident. An attacker with elevated privileges could modify or delete audit logs to hide evidence of unauthorized access or data exfiltration.
threats.mitigation: Implement dual-sink logging: Cloud Logging with organization-level log sink (immutable) plus BigQuery append-only audit tables. Apply hash-chaining on audit log entries for tamper evidence. Export logs to external SIEM within 60 seconds. Use separate security project with restricted IAM for log storage.

threats.description: IBM i and cloud audit log silos prevent unified audit trail. Security investigations require correlating events across two disconnected systems with different log formats and timezones.
threats.mitigation: Implement journal bridge via Precisely Connect audit stream to Pub/Sub, normalizing IBM i journal entries into structured JSON. Use BigQuery as the unified audit plane with standardized schema across both platforms. Assign X-Correlation-IDs at the Apigee gateway for cross-system event correlation.

threats.description: GenAI decision audit trail incomplete — no systematic capture of raw prompts, model responses, or model version metadata. Regulators cannot reconstruct the basis for AI-assisted PA decisions.
threats.mitigation: Deploy Vertex AI prediction logging wrapper that captures prompt text, model response, model version, and content hash for every inference call. Store in BigQuery with 3-year retention policy. Include pharmacist review decision and rationale in the same audit record. Index by correlation ID.

threats.description: Claims adjudication audit chain broken between IBM i and cloud — no correlation ID links the legacy claims processing steps to the cloud-based adjudication workflow.
threats.mitigation: Inject X-Correlation-ID at the Apigee gateway for every claims transaction. Propagate the correlation ID through IWS program calls via HTTP headers. Bridge IBM i journal entries to BigQuery via Precisely Connect with correlation ID field. Enable end-to-end trace reconstruction.

threats.description: PA review lacks step-up authentication — pharmacist identity is not re-verified at the decision commit point. A shared workstation session could allow unauthorized PA approvals.
threats.mitigation: Require step-up authentication (WebAuthn or TOTP) at the PA_REVIEW commit action. Apply digital signature on the decision record using the pharmacist's credential. Log the step-up authentication event in the audit trail alongside the PA decision.

threats.description: Controlled substance audit logs not tamper-evident — no hash chain mechanism to prove logs have not been altered after the fact, which is a DEA 21 CFR 1311 requirement.
threats.mitigation: Implement SHA-256 hash chaining for all controlled substance audit entries (each entry includes hash of previous entry). Store in Cloud Storage with bucket lock (WORM policy, minimum 3-year retention). Host in a separate organization security project with restricted IAM access.

threats.description: Clinical notes containing PHI enter the GenAI pipeline before the DLP gate processes them. A misconfigured pipeline DAG could route raw clinical text directly to the Vertex AI endpoint.
threats.mitigation: Configure Cloud DLP as a synchronous blocking gate in the pipeline DAG — inference step cannot execute until DLP returns clean status. Add post-inference DLP scan on model output before writing to ai_recommendations. Enforce VPC Service Controls to prevent data egress from the AI perimeter.

threats.description: Memorystore Redis cache contains PHI (member details, prescription data) in plaintext. Redis in-memory data is accessible to any service with network access and AUTH credentials.
threats.mitigation: Implement application-layer AES-256 encryption for all PHI fields before writing to Redis. Enforce AUTH password and TLS in-transit encryption on the Memorystore instance. Minimize cached PHI fields to only those required for session performance. Set TTL to 15 minutes for PHI cache entries.

threats.description: PHI leaking into application logs and error stack traces. Unstructured logging of request/response bodies or exception details could expose patient names, SSNs, or clinical data in Cloud Logging.
threats.mitigation: Deploy Cloud DLP log inspection on Cloud Logging sinks to detect PHI in log entries. Configure Apigee SensitiveDataMask policy on all API proxies. Implement structured logging middleware in all Cloud Run services with explicit PHI field redaction. Disable Apigee debug/trace sessions in production.

threats.description: PHI leaking during cross-cloud GCP-to-Azure data transfer for the myPBM integration surface (130+ APIs). Member and prescription data traverses cloud boundaries without consistent controls.
threats.mitigation: Apply PHI minimization at the GCP egress boundary — only transmit fields required by the specific myPBM API contract. Enforce mTLS with certificate pinning between Apigee X and Azure API Management. Maintain unified audit trail via Pub/Sub-to-Azure Event Hub log bridge for cross-cloud correlation.

threats.description: Vertex AI prompt injection causing PHI disclosure, or PHI contamination of evaluation datasets. Malicious prompts could extract training data or bypass output filters.
threats.mitigation: Enforce structured output via Gemini controlled generation (JSON schema, no free-text output fields). Apply pre-inference Cloud DLP scan on all assembled prompts. Use only synthetic clinical data for model evaluation — never real patient records. Red team prompt injection testing quarterly.

threats.description: DDoS attack against claims adjudication API endpoints which have a 500ms SLA. Volumetric or application-layer attacks could disrupt pharmacy operations nationwide.
threats.mitigation: Deploy Cloud Armor Advanced with Adaptive Protection ML for automatic DDoS detection and mitigation. Configure Apigee rate limiting (per-client and global quotas). Leverage Google global anycast network for volumetric attack absorption. Maintain pre-configured Cloud Armor security policies for emergency escalation.

threats.description: CDC pipeline outage causing data staleness beyond the 5-second lag SLA. Precisely Connect failure or Pub/Sub backpressure could leave cloud data stale while IBM i continues processing.
threats.mitigation: Deploy HA Precisely Connect instances with automatic failover. Implement circuit-breaker pattern with fallback to direct IBM i queries (IWS) when CDC lag exceeds threshold. Use separated Pub/Sub topics per data domain to isolate failures. Monitor CDC lag with Cloud Monitoring alerting at 3-second threshold.

threats.description: IBM i IWS thread pool exhaustion from cloud-originated API traffic. Cloud services could overwhelm the limited IWS thread pool, blocking both cloud and legacy workstation users.
threats.mitigation: Deploy a dedicated connection broker proxy with fixed-size thread pool matching IWS capacity. Configure Apigee backend target limits to cap concurrent connections to the IBM i. Set IWS RequestReadTimeout and ResponseWriteTimeout to prevent hung connections. Reserve IWS threads for legacy workstation priority traffic.

threats.description: Partner Interconnect bandwidth saturation at 1-2 Gbps capacity during peak claims processing. Concurrent CDC replication, API traffic, and batch operations could exceed link capacity.
threats.mitigation: Plan upgrade path to Dedicated Interconnect (10 Gbps) for Phase 2. Implement QoS with DSCP marking to prioritize claims API traffic over batch operations. Cache frequently accessed IBM i data in Memorystore to reduce cross-link queries. Monitor bandwidth utilization with alerting at 70% threshold.

threats.description: Cloud SQL connection pool exhaustion from concurrent Cloud Run service instances. Auto-scaling Cloud Run could spawn more instances than the database connection limit supports.
threats.mitigation: Enable Cloud SQL Managed Connection Pooling (PgBouncer) to multiplex application connections. Deploy read replicas for read-heavy workloads (formulary lookups, member queries). Set per-service connection limits in Cloud Run environment configuration. Monitor active connections with Cloud SQL Insights.

threats.description: IBM i *ALLOBJ and *SECADM special authorities mapped to cloud IAM without downscoping, granting excessive cloud privileges. Legacy system administrators could gain unintended access to cloud resources.
threats.mitigation: Create mandatory authority-to-IAM translation matrix reviewed by security team. Apply FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all Cloud SQL tables. Re-own IWS service programs to dedicated service profiles with minimal authority. Audit all *ALLOBJ/*SECADM profiles and map to least-privilege cloud roles.

threats.description: ABAC attribute falsification — users manipulate claims attributes (DEA certification status, HIPAA training completion) to gain access to controlled substance workflows or PHI they should not see.
threats.mitigation: Source all ABAC attributes server-side from the authoritative HR system — never accept client-supplied attribute claims. Embed attributes in non-malleable JWT claims signed by Cloud Identity Platform. Set 8-hour cache TTL with forced re-evaluation for sensitive operations. Audit attribute changes in real time.

threats.description: Cloud SQL Row-Level Security circumvention through SQL injection or use of BYPASSRLS privilege. An attacker could access PHI records across organizational boundaries.
threats.mitigation: Apply FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all PHI tables (prevents BYPASSRLS override). Use parameterized queries exclusively — no string concatenation for SQL. Authenticate database connections via Cloud SQL IAM authentication (no shared passwords). Run quarterly SQL injection penetration testing.

threats.description: Service account over-provisioning with project-level IAM roles (roles/editor, roles/owner). Compromised service accounts could access all resources in the project.
threats.mitigation: Replace all project-level role bindings with resource-level bindings (specific Cloud SQL instances, Pub/Sub topics, etc.). Use Workload Identity Federation instead of service account keys. Enable iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation org policy. Run IAM Recommender weekly to identify over-provisioned accounts.

threats.description: GenAI pipeline service account (genai-pipeline@) accessing raw PHI before DLP processing. A misconfigured IAM binding could allow the AI pipeline to read clinical data directly from Cloud SQL or FHIR Store.
threats.mitigation: Enforce IAM separation: genai-pipeline@ has zero IAM bindings on Cloud SQL or FHIR Store resources. Create dedicated DLP gate service account that reads raw PHI and writes de-identified output to a separate bucket. Enforce VPC Service Controls to prevent genai-pipeline@ from accessing PHI perimeter.

network_perimeter.description: Network-layer controls forming the outermost security boundary
controls: VPC Service Controls perimeter around all HIPAA-regulated services (Cloud SQL, FHIR Store, BigQuery, Vertex AI, Cloud Storage)
Partner Interconnect with IPsec encryption for IBM i hybrid connectivity (1-2 Gbps, upgrade path to Dedicated Interconnect at 10 Gbps)
HA VPN (IPsec) as backup connectivity for redundancy and failover
Cloud Armor WAF with OWASP Top 10 managed rules and Adaptive Protection ML-based DDoS mitigation
Dedicated VLANs per traffic type: API traffic, CDC replication, management/monitoring
Firewall rules: deny-all default with explicit allow per service-to-service path
No public IP addresses on Cloud Run, Cloud SQL, or Memorystore instances

identity_and_access.description: Identity verification and access management layer
controls: Cloud Identity Platform federating with Active Directory and Okta via SAML 2.0 and OIDC
OAuth 2.0 + OIDC for user authentication; SMART on FHIR for clinical data access
Hybrid RBAC (7 roles: PHARMACY_TECH, PHARMACIST, CLINICAL_PHARMACIST, PLAN_ADMIN, FORMULARY_MANAGER, AUDITOR, SYSTEM_ADMIN) + ABAC (DEA certification, department, location, time-of-day)
Step-up authentication (WebAuthn/TOTP) for PHI access and controlled substance operations
5 dedicated service accounts with resource-level IAM bindings (bff-service@, genai-pipeline@, cdc-pipeline@, apigee-runtime@, monitoring-sa@)
Zero trust architecture following BeyondCorp principles — verify explicitly, assume breach
Workload Identity Federation for all service-to-service authentication (no JSON keys)
Input validation via Apigee JSON Threat Protection policy and OpenAPI schema enforcement with bounds checking
Content Security Policy (CSP) headers on React frontend — script-src 'self', no inline scripts
JWT validation centralized at Apigee X gateway only — no application-layer JWT parsing
Structured logging middleware with explicit PHI field redaction in all Cloud Run services
OWASP Top 10 for web applications and OWASP Top 10 for LLMs applied across all services
Apigee SensitiveDataMask policy on all API proxies to prevent PHI in debug/trace output
Dependency scanning via Artifact Registry vulnerability scanning for all container images
CMEK encryption (AES-256) with separate Cloud KMS key rings per data classification: PHI, operational, AI artifacts
TLS 1.2+ enforced for all data in transit between cloud services
IPsec encryption over Partner Interconnect for IBM i hybrid connectivity
Cloud DLP for automated PHI detection and de-identification before AI processing
BigQuery column-level security for PHI fields with IAM-based access control
Cloud SQL Row-Level Security (FORCE RLS) for multi-tenant PHI isolation
SSN stored as SHA-256 hash only in cloud — no plaintext SSN in any cloud data store
Application-layer AES-256 encryption for PHI cached in Memorystore Redis

monitoring_and_audit.description: Continuous monitoring, audit logging, and threat detection
controls: Cloud Monitoring + Cloud Logging + Cloud Trace + OpenTelemetry for full observability stack
VPC Flow Logs enabled on all subnets for network traffic analysis
HIPAA audit logging with 6-year retention policy (Cloud Logging + BigQuery)
Cloud Security Command Center Premium for vulnerability scanning and threat detection
SIEM integration via Pub/Sub export for real-time security event correlation
Anomaly detection on authentication patterns, PHI access frequency, and API usage
Hash-chained controlled substance audit logs with WORM storage policy

bff-service@.purpose: Backend-for-Frontend Cloud Run service handling user-facing API requests
bindings: roles/pubsub.publisher on pa-events and claims-events topics
bff-service@.restrictions: No Vertex AI access, no Cloud Storage access, no BigQuery access

genai-pipeline@.purpose: GenAI PA recommendation pipeline — processes only de-identified data
genai-pipeline@.restrictions: NO Cloud SQL bindings, NO FHIR Store bindings, NO raw PHI storage access. Enforced by VPC-SC perimeter.

cdc-pipeline@.purpose: Change Data Capture pipeline processing IBM i journal entries
bindings: roles/pubsub.publisher on cdc-claims, cdc-formulary, cdc-member topics
cdc-pipeline@.restrictions: No direct Cloud SQL write access, no Vertex AI access

apigee-runtime@.purpose: Apigee X runtime service account for API gateway operations
bindings: roles/run.invoker on specific Cloud Run services
apigee-runtime@.restrictions: No data store access, no Vertex AI access

monitoring-sa@.purpose: Monitoring and observability — read-only access to metrics and logs
monitoring-sa@.restrictions: NO admin roles, NO write access, NO data store access. Viewer-only permissions.

user_roles.PHARMACY_TECH: Claims lookup, prescription status, basic member info. No PA decisions, no formulary changes.
user_roles.PHARMACIST: PA review and approval, prescription verification, drug interaction review. Step-up auth for PA decisions.
user_roles.CLINICAL_PHARMACIST: Senior PA review (low-confidence AI recommendations), clinical override authority, formulary input.
user_roles.PLAN_ADMIN: Plan configuration, benefit design, member eligibility management. No clinical operations.
user_roles.FORMULARY_MANAGER: Formulary tier management, drug list updates, PA criteria configuration. Dual-control approval required.
user_roles.AUDITOR: Read-only access to audit logs, compliance reports, and anonymized analytics. No PHI access.
user_roles.SYSTEM_ADMIN: Infrastructure management, IAM administration, security configuration. No direct PHI access — break-glass only.

authentication.session_management: JWT access tokens (15-minute lifetime), refresh tokens (8-hour lifetime), session stored in Memorystore Redis with AES-256 encryption

network.waf: Cloud Armor Advanced with Adaptive Protection

encryption.at_rest: AES-256 CMEK via Cloud KMS (separate key rings per data classification)
encryption.in_transit: TLS 1.2+ for cloud services, IPsec for Partner Interconnect
encryption.key_rotation: 90-day automated rotation for CMEK, 30-day for service credentials
encryption.field_level: Application-layer AES-256 for PHI in Redis, SHA-256 hash-only for SSN

security_architecture.secrets_management: Cloud KMS for CMEK, Secret Manager for API keys and credentials, HashiCorp Vault for IBM i connection credentials. No JSON keys — Workload Identity Federation for service-to-service auth.

logging_and_monitoring.retention: 6 years for HIPAA audit logs, 3 years for AI decision logs, 1 year for operational logs

prompt_injection_protection: Cloud DLP pre-inference scan on assembled prompt — block on PHI detection before any data reaches the model
Structured output enforcement via Gemini controlled generation (JSON schema, no free-text fields)
Input sanitization: user-supplied clinical notes wrapped in XML delimiters with security instructions preventing prompt override
Red team prompt injection testing quarterly using JAMA Network Open adversarial test suite
output_filtering: Post-inference Cloud DLP scan on model output before writing to ai_recommendations table
Confidence threshold >= 0.8 for standard pharmacist queue; all below route to senior clinical pharmacist
Ensemble disagreement detection: Gemini (primary) vs MedGemma (secondary) — divergence triggers escalation to manual review
Structured output schema prohibits free-text fields where PHI could leak into downstream systems
ai_security_controls.model_access_control: genai-pipeline@ scoped to specific Vertex AI endpoint via VPC-SC + IAM. No direct FHIR or Cloud SQL access. Model artifacts stored with CMEK. Model access audit via Cloud Audit Logs.
ai_security_controls.data_poisoning_prevention: No PHI used for model training or fine-tuning (Vertex AI walled garden). Evaluation framework uses synthetic clinical data, not real patient notes. RLHF feedback loop restricted to credentialed clinical pharmacists with MFA.
ai_security_controls.hallucination_mitigation: RAG-based grounding against formulary database and drug interaction databases. Citations required in every recommendation. Evaluation framework scores groundedness (0-1). HITL pharmacist review mandatory for all PA decisions — no autonomous AI actions.

compliance_mapping.evidence: Cloud Identity Platform with RBAC (7 roles) + ABAC, Cloud SQL RLS, VPC Service Controls perimeter
compliance_mapping.notes: Access controls enforced at API gateway (Apigee), service (Cloud Run), and data (Cloud SQL RLS) layers

compliance_mapping.evidence: Cloud Identity Platform assigns unique identifiers; federated with AD/Okta for enterprise identity
compliance_mapping.notes: All users uniquely identified via OIDC subject claim; service accounts uniquely named per service

compliance_mapping.evidence: Break-glass access procedure designed with GCP PAM workflow and JIT access grants
compliance_mapping.notes: Design complete but not yet implemented in Cloud Identity Platform. See F-002.

compliance_mapping.evidence: CMEK AES-256 via Cloud KMS for all PHI at rest; separate key rings per data classification
compliance_mapping.notes: PHI key ring, operational key ring, and AI artifacts key ring with 90-day rotation

compliance_mapping.evidence: Cloud Audit Logs on all PHI-containing services, BigQuery audit trail, 6-year retention
compliance_mapping.notes: Dual-sink logging (Cloud Logging + BigQuery) with hash-chained entries for tamper evidence

compliance_mapping.evidence: HMAC-SHA256 on CDC messages, JWS signing on claims payloads, event sourcing for formulary changes
compliance_mapping.notes: Data integrity verified at ingestion, processing, and storage boundaries

compliance_mapping.evidence: MFA required for all PHI access (TOTP minimum, FIDO2 for controlled substances), step-up auth for PA decisions
compliance_mapping.notes: WebAuthn/FIDO2 for pharmacist PA review commit; biometric option for mobile workflows

compliance_mapping.evidence: TLS 1.2+ for all cloud services, IPsec over Partner Interconnect, mTLS for cross-cloud transfers
compliance_mapping.notes: No unencrypted data transmission paths exist in the architecture

compliance_mapping.evidence: TLS 1.2+ enforced on all endpoints, IPsec for hybrid connectivity, certificate pinning for cross-cloud
compliance_mapping.notes: Cloud Armor enforces minimum TLS version; HSTS headers on all public endpoints

compliance_mapping.evidence: BigQuery audit tables with 6-year retention policy, Cloud Storage with lifecycle rules, WORM for controlled substances
compliance_mapping.notes: Retention policies enforced via BigQuery table expiration and Cloud Storage bucket lifecycle

compliance_mapping.evidence: Incident response plan designed with detection, containment, and notification procedures
compliance_mapping.notes: IR plan documented but notification workflow automation and HHS reporting procedures pending implementation

compliance_mapping.evidence: Payment card data tokenized at point of capture; no PAN stored in cloud; CMEK encryption on all data stores
compliance_mapping.notes: Pharmacy payment processing uses tokenization gateway; cloud stores only tokenized references

compliance_mapping.evidence: MFA on all payment system access, unique user IDs, Cloud Identity Platform federation, session timeout controls
compliance_mapping.notes: Payment system access requires RBAC role with PCI scope and active MFA session

compliance_mapping.evidence: EPCS identity verification service designed with CSP integration architecture
compliance_mapping.notes: DEA-approved Credential Service Provider (Imprivata, Exostar) integration designed but not yet contracted. See F-003.

compliance_mapping.evidence: Tamper-evident SHA-256 hash chain designed for controlled substance prescriptions
compliance_mapping.notes: Hash chain architecture designed and validated; implementation pending Phase 1 build

compliance_mapping.evidence: Controlled substance audit log design with hash chaining and WORM storage
compliance_mapping.notes: Audit log schema and retention policies defined; implementation and DEA audit readiness pending

compliance_mapping.requirement: CC6.1 Logical and Physical Access Controls
compliance_mapping.evidence: Cloud IAM with resource-level bindings, VPC Service Controls, firewall deny-all default, Workload Identity Federation
compliance_mapping.notes: Logical access controls enforced at network, identity, application, and data layers

compliance_mapping.requirement: CC6.2 Prior to Issuing System Credentials
compliance_mapping.evidence: Identity governance design includes provisioning workflow and access review process
compliance_mapping.notes: IGA tool selection pending (SailPoint, Saviynt, ConductorOne evaluation). See F-008.

compliance_mapping.evidence: 7 RBAC roles with ABAC attributes, enforced at Apigee, Cloud Run, and Cloud SQL layers
compliance_mapping.notes: Role definitions aligned with job functions; ABAC provides fine-grained contextual access control

compliance_mapping.requirement: CC7.2 Monitor System Components for Anomalies
compliance_mapping.evidence: Cloud Security Command Center Premium, VPC Flow Logs, anomaly detection on auth and PHI access patterns, SIEM integration
compliance_mapping.notes: Real-time alerting on anomalous patterns with Pub/Sub export to external SIEM

compliance_mapping.evidence: BeyondCorp principles adopted: verify explicitly, least privilege, assume breach. VPC-SC, IAM, mTLS implemented.
compliance_mapping.notes: Core Zero Trust controls in place. Full implementation (device posture checks, continuous evaluation) planned across phases.

must: Least-privilege access on all service accounts (resource-level, never project-level)
All secrets in Secret Manager or Vault — never in code, environment variables, or container images
Audit logging on all PHI access with 6-year retention (HIPAA §164.530(j))
Content guardrails for GenAI: Cloud DLP blocking gate before and after inference
Encrypt everything: AES-256 CMEK at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit, IPsec over Partner Interconnect
MFA required for all users accessing PHI (TOTP minimum, FIDO2 for controlled substances)
Step-up authentication for PA review decisions and formulary modifications
FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on all Cloud SQL tables containing PHI
Document security architecture decisions with threat rationale
must_not: Hardcode credentials or API keys in source code or container images
Store PHI in Redis/Memorystore without application-layer encryption
Use service account JSON keys — use Workload Identity Federation instead
Grant project-level roles/editor or roles/owner to service accounts
Log PHI in application logs, error traces, or Apigee debug sessions
Use production PHI data in test environments or AI model training
Deploy without security testing (SAST, DAST, penetration testing)
Allow direct Cloud Run access bypassing Apigee X gateway (set ingress=internal-and-cloud-load-balancing)
Store the Supabase service_role key in client-accessible environment variables (Option C)
Skip Cloud DLP de-identification step in the GenAI pipeline for any reason

open_items.finding: IBM i journal bridge for unified audit trail not yet implemented — audit logs remain siloed between IBM i and GCP
open_items.recommendation: Implement Precisely Connect audit stream to Pub/Sub before dual-mode operation begins

open_items.finding: Break-glass access procedure designed but not yet implemented in Cloud Identity Platform
open_items.recommendation: Implement GCP PAM workflow with JIT access before go-live

open_items.finding: EPCS verification service for DEA-controlled substance identity proofing needs CSP integration
open_items.recommendation: Integrate with DEA-approved CSP (Imprivata, Exostar) in Phase 1

open_items.finding: Cross-cloud audit bridge (GCP to Azure myPBM) not yet designed
open_items.recommendation: Implement Pub/Sub to Azure Event Hub log forwarding for unified cross-cloud audit trail

open_items.finding: IBM i IWS service programs not yet audited for adopted authority exposure
open_items.recommendation: Run SECTOOLS audit on all IWS-exposed programs, re-own privileged programs to dedicated service profiles

open_items.finding: DLP template coverage for PBM-specific PHI patterns (NCPDP codes, RxBIN) not yet validated
open_items.recommendation: Create custom DLP info types for pharmacy identifiers and validate detection rates

open_items.finding: Quarterly DR failover drill procedure not yet documented
open_items.recommendation: Create and execute first DR drill before claims adjudication migration
